title: Windows: `--runner codex` fails with spaced prompts due to cmd /c wrapping
author: Dteyn

stage1.summary: Real bug. On Windows, _resolve_executable() in codex_batch.py always wraps through cmd /c, even for .exe binaries. _wrap_cmd_c() tries to fix quoting by collapsing the inner command via subprocess.list2cmdline(), but when subprocess.Popen calls list2cmdline again on the outer list, prompts containing spaces get double-quoted with backslash escapes that cmd.exe doesn't understand. This produces the exact 'unexpected argument' errors the reporter describes. The fix is straightforward: only use cmd /c for .cmd/.bat wrappers, invoke .exe binaries directly.
stage1.suggested_fix: In _resolve_executable(), check if the resolved path ends with .exe — if so, return [resolved] directly without cmd /c wrapping. Only use cmd /c for .cmd/.bat shims. This is a ~5-line change to _resolve_executable() and may also simplify or eliminate _wrap_cmd_c().

stage3.reasoning: Both stages agreed the bug is real. Fix is minimal: _resolve_executable() now skips cmd /c for .exe binaries, only using it for .cmd/.bat shims and unresolved fallback. Kept _wrap_cmd_c() intact for the .cmd/.bat path. Added unit tests covering all branches.
changes_applied: Only changed _resolve_executable() — did not restructure _wrap_cmd_c() or the broader Windows codepath
Added unit tests for _resolve_executable() covering .exe, .cmd, .bat, unresolved, and non-Windows cases
Added integration test verifying .exe path produces no cmd /c in codex_batch_command output
